factors.name: Geopolitical De-escalation (Iran Deal)
factors.summary: Trump announced framework deal with Iran, canceling military strikes and signaling weekend signing. This removes the primary safe-haven bid that drove gold to $4,250+, creating near-term headwind despite longer-term relief narrative.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY rallied to 99.80 on hot US PPI data and Middle East uncertainty (FXStreet, 02:42 UTC). Stronger USD directly competes with gold pricing and pressures XAUUSD—the inverse correlation is critical here.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit 10y TIPS data in newsflow, but hot US PPI (May wholesale prices above consensus per FXStreet 01:56 UTC) implies real yields may have risen slightly, weighing on gold. This is the single largest structural headwind.

factors.name: Inflation Data (US PPI)
factors.summary: US May PPI came in hotter than expected (FXStreet 01:56, 02:42 UTC), lifting USD and tempering expectations for near-term rate cuts. Higher inflation prints typically sell gold in immediate aftermath as they reduce real-yield appeal.

factors.name: Risk-On Sentiment Shift
factors.summary: Equity markets surged (Nikkei +4%, Kospi +8%, Al Jazeera 00:47) on Iran deal hopes, indicating risk-on rotation away from safe havens. Gold's safe-haven premium is compressing as equities rally.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI dropped to $85/bbl (from ~$90+) on deal optimism (Bloomberg 22:03, FXStreet 01:59). Lower oil removes inflation hedging argument and signals deflation expectations, structurally headwind for gold.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy (RBA, BoK)
factors.summary: RBA widely expected to hold at 4.35% on June 16 per Reuters poll (Investinglive 03:04). South Korea's BoK governor flagged potential rate hikes as early as next month (Investinglive 01:06). Mixed signals, no immediate directional impact.

factors.name: ECB Rate Hike
factors.summary: ECB raised rates for first time since 2023, supporting EUR/USD strength near 1.1575 (Bloomberg 00:03, FXStreet 00:06). This eases some USD pressure and supports gold via softer real-rate expectations abroad.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Physical Demand
factors.summary: No explicit GLD/IAU flow data in newsflow. Bloomberg noted gold held gains but made no mention of institutional repositioning; Indian/Chinese seasonality not yet a driver in June.

factors.name: BoJ Policy & Yen Carry
factors.summary: BoJ monetary policy meeting June 15-16 proceeds as planned despite Governor Ueda's hospitalization (FXStreet 01:21). USD/JPY near 160.25 on hot PPI (FXStreet 01:10). No major hawkish surprise signal yet; carry trade remains intact but not aggressively unwinding.

screening.narrative: Gold rallied +2.94% in 24h to $4,224 on Iran deal fears and safe-haven bid, but the move is now in PHASE_2 consolidation as three headwinds collide: (1) hot US PPI data lifting real yields and USD/DXY to 99.80, (2) equity risk-on rotation (Nikkei +4%, Kospi +8%) siphoning safe-haven demand, and (3) oil crash to $85 removing inflation hedging appeal. Trump's Iran deal announcement is real but not yet signed—markets are treating it cautiously (Iran Supreme Leader approval uncertain per Investinglive 23:06). The narrative has shifted from geopolitical premium to macro headwinds.
catalysts_ahead: BoJ policy decision + forward guidance, June 15–16 (potential surprise hawkish shift)
RBA decision June 16 + forward guidance (widely expected hold, but tone matters for AUD, risk sentiment)
US inflation data (PCE, Core PCE) — no specific date in newsflow but typically mid-to-late month (major real-yield driver)
Iran deal formal signing (weekend per Trump claim, but unconfirmed; if falls through, sharp re-rally in gold/oil)
US economic data (NFP, ISM, retail sales) — no dates specified but ongoing labor market strength will support hawkish Fed view
Powell/Fed speakers — no near-term schedule in newsflow
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal formally collapses or Supreme Leader rejects framework, gold will spike back to $4,250+ on renewed geopolitical premium and risk-off. Conversely, if deal is fully signed AND US economic data continues to support Fed hold/eventual hike, real yields could rise further and push DXY to 100+, crushing gold to $4,100–$4,050. Equities correction would also reverse the risk-on trade and reignite safe-haven bid. BoJ surprise hawkish tilt on June 15 would materially strengthen yen carry unwind and USD, adding downside pressure.
screening.entry_guidance: Move extended from $4,150 (pre-spike low) to current $4,224 in 24h; PHASE_2 consolidation suggests pullback entry near $4,200–$4,190 support preferable to chasing into resistance. If Iran deal formally signed, expect further weakness toward $4,180–$4,150; if deal fails, reversal above $4,280 becomes likely. Monitor DXY and real-yield proxies (10y nominal Treasury yield) for next directional signal.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY advancing and holding gains near 99.80 amid prevailing risk aversion and hawkish Fed outlook. Strong USD is directly bearish for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Not explicitly stated in news, but implied by US rate expectations and hot inflation surprises; higher nominal yields with sticky inflation typically support real yields, supporting bearish gold pressure.

factors.name: US Inflation Data (PPI)
factors.summary: Hot US PPI data released, bolstering USD strength and supporting expectations of potential Fed rate maintenance. Inflation surprise typically bearish for gold in near term.

factors.name: Geopolitical De-escalation (Iran-US Deal)
factors.summary: Trump claims framework deal nearing with Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz and lift oil sanctions. Market repricing away Middle East risk premium; Brent crude fell below $90/bbl. Reduced geopolitical premium removes safe-haven bid for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Brent crude collapsed below $90/bbl on Iran deal hopes, down from ~$100+. Lower energy prices reduce inflation expectations, diminishing gold's inflation-hedge appeal and supporting real yield elevation.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equities
factors.summary: S&P 500 posting largest gain in two months on lower oil and reduced Fed hike expectations; global equities rallied on Iran de-escalation. Rising equities typically compete with gold for risk capital.

factors.name: ECB Policy (Recent Hike)
factors.summary: ECB hiked 25 bps as expected; policymakers signalled potential pause in July if oil doesn't rise further. Lagarde avoided clear forward guidance, leaving EUR softer. Supportive of DXY strength relative to EUR.

factors.name: Gold Technical Position
factors.summary: Gold 'remains depressed below $4,200' per FXStreet, holding above lowest level since November but under key resistance. Recent 4h +0.72% and 24h +0.31% gains suggest limited upside momentum after extended weakness.

factors.name: Prior Bias Alignment
factors.summary: Previous read (2026-06-12 03:44) was BUY 6/10 with no confirmation triggered, suggesting weak conviction. Today's de-escalation news represents material shift toward bearish case — first-cycle reaction from new catalyst.

screening.narrative: Gold is in a structural bear phase driven by a confluence of real yield pressure, USD strength, and geopolitical de-escalation. The Iran-US deal framework announcement (still unconfirmed but market-pricing in via crude collapse below $90/bbl) has gutted the Middle East risk premium and shifted sentiment away from safe-haven assets. Simultaneously, hot US PPI data is supporting Fed resolve to hold or remain hawkish, keeping nominal and real yields elevated. Equities rallying on lower oil and reduced inflation expectations further redirects capital away from gold. While gold has bounced modestly in recent sessions (+0.72% 4h), it remains capped below $4,200 technical resistance and faces headwinds from a stronger dollar and diminished geopolitical urgency.
catalysts_ahead: Weekend Iran-US deal finalization (or credible denial) — key retest of geopolitical repricing
Fed speakers (Warsh as incoming Fed Chair could signal policy direction)
ECB July rate guidance confirmation — EUR weakness could extend DXY gains
Oil price action (Brent target: $80–$85 if Hormuz reopens; $150+ if still closed) — direct macro and gold inflation-link signal
US economic data flow: retail sales, ISM, jobless claims (inflation/growth expectations)
BoJ policy communication on intervention / yen carry unwind (USD/JPY above 160 is intervention risk)
screening.risk_warning: Iran deal collapses or stalls → geopolitical premium snaps back, oil spikes, safe-haven demand resurges, gold breaks above $4,250. Alternatively, if Fed unexpectedly signals rate cuts (soft employment data, credit stress) → real yields collapse, gold re-accelerates higher. Structural BoJ intervention or global risk-off event could also flip bias to BULLISH. Monitor Brent and DXY intraday — if either reverses sharply, gold reversal likely follows within 24–48h.
screening.entry_guidance: Move in progress (phase 2): consider short entries on retest of $4,230–$4,250 resistance with tight stops above $4,270. Do not chase lower; wait for pullback before re-entry. If gold breaks below $4,180 support on sustained selling, trend acceleration likely; consider larger position. Key risk: weekend geopolitical headlines or surprise dovish central bank signal could invalidate.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk premium unwinding
factors.summary: U.S.-Iran deal framework announced by Trump, with proposed MoU to reopen Strait of Hormuz and lift oil sanctions. Markets pricing lower war-risk premium despite Iran saying "nothing finalized" and confusion over actual terms. Classic safe-haven unwind underway.

factors.name: Oil prices collapsing
factors.summary: Brent fell below $90/bbl per Societe Generale and ING reports on ceasefire hopes. Lower oil = reduced inflation expectations = reduced gold appeal as inflation hedge. FGE's Fesharaki warned of $150+ oil if Hormuz stays closed, but market pricing opposite scenario now.

factors.name: U.S. Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD Index advancing to 99.80 per FXStreet on hot U.S. PPI data (referenced in multiple articles) and hawkish Fed repricing. Stronger dollar mechanically pressures gold. EUR/USD pulling back from 1.1590 weekly highs to 1.1565.

factors.name: Inflation data surprise
factors.summary: Hot U.S. PPI referenced across multiple FX articles (NZD/USD, CAD/USD mentions) lifted Fed hike expectations. European inflation also accelerating: French CPI +2.4% y/y vs +2.2% prior, Spanish CPI holding at 3.2%, German core CPI +2.5% vs +2.3% prior. ECB hiked 25bps and Nagel says "ready to hike again in July if necessary."

factors.name: Federal Reserve policy path
factors.summary: Deutsche Bank notes "reduced Fed hike expectations" initially helped equities, but hot PPI has reversed that. Markets now pricing higher for longer. Bloomberg references incoming Fed Chair Kevin Warsh navigating "combination of higher energy prices and surprisingly strong US jobs report." Net hawkish tilt pressures gold.

factors.name: Real yields (implied)
factors.summary: No direct TIPS yield quoted, but combination of hot inflation data + hawkish Fed repricing + rising nominal yields (per DB report on "rates repricing") suggests real yields rising or stable at elevated levels. This is gold's primary headwind.

factors.name: Risk sentiment mixed
factors.summary: Equities rallied on Iran deal hopes (S&P 500 largest gain in two months per Deutsche Bank), but futures now softening (Nasdaq -0.29%, S&P -0.09%). Gold losing safe-haven bid but not getting risk-on support. VIX not mentioned but equity rally suggests compression.

factors.name: Central bank buying absent
factors.summary: No fresh news on PBoC, RBI, or other major CB gold purchases in this news cycle. This structural bid is dormant in current reporting window.

factors.name: Positioning and technicals
factors.summary: FXStreet reports gold "remains depressed below $4,200" and "holds above the lowest level since November, touched the previous day." Price at $4,224.80 is only +0.31% in 24h after testing key lows. Momentum clearly negative, testing major support zone.

factors.name: Cross-asset flows
factors.summary: Japanese Yen weakening sharply (USD/JPY returning above 160.00 intervention level), suggesting yen carry unwind reversing. Indian Rupee rebounding on lower oil. Flows moving away from safe havens and back toward risk/carry trades.

key_levels.support: $4,200 (psychological and recent multi-month low from yesterday)
key_levels.resistance: $4,250-4,260 (would need geopolitical escalation or major USD reversal)

confirmation.narrative: Gold is caught in a violent safe-haven unwind as markets price Trump's claimed Iran deal framework, despite Tehran's pushback that "nothing is finalized." The combination of collapsing oil prices (Brent <$90), hot U.S. and European inflation data driving hawkish central bank repricing (ECB hiked, ready to go again in July; Fed expectations rising on hot PPI), and broad USD strength is creating a perfect storm for gold bears. The $4,200 level tested yesterday represents the lowest since November and is now critical support. However, the deal confusion and continued Middle East uncertainty (IRGC vowing "regret-inducing response," U.S. downing Iranian drones per wrap) creates whipsaw risk.
catalysts_ahead: Clarity on actual U.S.-Iran deal terms and Iran leadership approval (could reverse safe-haven unwind instantly if talks collapse)
Any Fed speaker commentary on inflation trajectory given hot PPI
Weekend geopolitical developments - actual Hormuz reopening or renewed escalation
Next week: RBA meeting June 16 (hold expected but AUD/rates sensitive), SpaceX IPO Friday could shift broader risk sentiment
confirmation.risk_warning: Immediate invalidation if Iran formally rejects deal or Trump reverses course again (classic boy-who-cried-wolf pattern). Any Hormuz incident or oil spike above $95 would trigger fast safe-haven bid back into gold. Also, if USD Index fails to hold 99.50 or U.S. yields reverse lower, gold could bounce sharply from oversold $4,200 support. The deal uncertainty is binary risk.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already underway in Phase 2; gold down from recent highs and testing key $4,200 support. Wait for a relief bounce toward $4,240-4,250 resistance before considering shorts, or confirmation break below $4,200 on volume with Iran deal formalization. Avoid chasing at current levels given whipsaw risk into weekend.

reason: DISAGREEMENT: Haiku=SELL vs Sonnet=BEARISH — silent log

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Hopes
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports US-Iran MoU could be signed by Sunday in Geneva, easing Middle East geopolitical premium. Oil prices plunging (WTI down ~2% to $82.90) as risk-off trade unwinds, traditionally negative for gold's safe-haven bid. This is the dominant market narrative driving risk-on sentiment.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No specific 10y TIPS data released in this window. Market sentiment on real yields is mixed: US inflation resilience (per MUFG: 'higher-for-longer backdrop') suggests sticky real yields, but potential geopolitical de-escalation could ease inflation expectations slightly. Without fresh data, signal is ambiguous.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy & Dot Plot
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting is June 17 (5 days away). Nordea expects 'more neutral policy stance' with rate cut projections likely removed from dot plot. No surprise here; markets pricing 18 bps rate hikes by year-end per investingLive data. No major hawkish/dovish shock yet, but Warsh debut is looming catalyst.

factors.name: ECB Rate Hike (June 12)
factors.summary: Deutsche Bank reports ECB delivered first rate hike since 2023, raising deposit rate to 2.25% with hawkish Lagarde messaging. This tightens EUR and pulls capital to Eurozone, potentially strengthening EUR/USD and weighing on USD-denominated commodities. Mild headwind for gold.

factors.name: USD Index & EUR/USD
factors.summary: EUR/USD trading flat around 1.1575 per FXStreet; BBH expects drift toward 1.1400 as US growth outperforms. ECB hike initially supported EUR but US data resilience (per MUFG) and tariff/growth narrative favour USD strength. Stronger USD is BEARISH for gold (inverse correlation).

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: WTI down ~2% to $82.90 on US-Iran deal optimism, unwinding Hormuz strait risk premium. Lower oil = lower inflation expectations, which typically pressures gold. Oil is both an inflation input and a geopolitical risk barometer; its decline signals risk-off in macro uncertainty, negative for safe-haven gold.

factors.name: Equity Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: S&P 500 futures up 0.6% on Iran deal hopes and SpaceX IPO euphoria ($75B record deal). VIX implied lower on risk-on rotation. Risk-on equity flows compete with gold; when equities rally on geopolitical relief, gold typically underperforms (loss of safe-haven bid).

factors.name: Inflation Data (Latest)
factors.summary: India CPI came in at 3.93% YoY in May vs 4.0% expected (slightly cooler per FXStreet). No fresh US CPI/PCE in this window (next major print likely mid-month). Mixed signals on sticky inflation; no game-changer release yet.

factors.name: BoJ Policy & JPY Carry Trade
factors.summary: InvestingLive shows 90% probability of BoJ rate hike at next meeting, with 46 bps hikes expected by year-end. This would tighten yen carry unwind, potentially supporting JPY/USD and creating some equity/commodity volatility. Not yet priced as shock; monitoring.

factors.name: Gold Technical & Price Action
factors.summary: Gold recovered from $4,023 low (7-month nadir) to hold near $4,200-$4,228. FXStreet notes 'mean-reversion move to 20-day EMA looks likely,' suggesting consolidation. 4h change -0.34%, 24h change +0.39% indicates choppy, directionless price action—no momentum break yet.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: US-Iran deal framework announced; Strait of Hormuz reopening expected imminently. Israel-Lebanon tensions ongoing but contained vs prior weeks. Ukraine/Russia static. Overall: geopolitical tail risk is DECREASING, which removes a key support pillar for gold's safe-haven bid.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No fresh PBoC, RBI, or other CB gold purchase data in this news window. Structural long-term bid remains, but does not offset short-term cyclical bearish drivers from risk-on sentiment and stronger USD.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows
factors.summary: No specific GLD/IAU holdings data released in past 24h. Institutional positioning unknown; likely waiting for clarity post-Iran deal and post-Warsh FOMC debut.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a risk-on unwind triggered by imminent US-Iran peace deal hopes. Oil prices crashing (~$82.90 WTI), equities rallying on SpaceX IPO euphoria and geopolitical relief, and USD strengthening on ECB hawkishness vs Fed dovishness all conspire against gold's safe-haven appeal. The 24h consolidation (+0.39%) masks underlying bearish pressure: real yields likely sticky, DXY likely firm, and geopolitical tail risk collapsing. However, Warsh's debut (June 17) and persistent 'higher-for-longer' US inflation narrative (per MUFG) prevent a clean sell signal. Market is in wait-and-see mode before next major catalyst.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran MoU signature in Geneva (expected by Sunday, June 15)
Fed's Kevin Warsh FOMC debut (June 17) — dot plot and forward guidance critical
Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index preliminary (June 13-14)
US weekly jobless claims (Thursday/Friday typical)
Oil price follow-through on Iran deal terms (structural bid support if deal confirmed)
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran deal fails or is delayed past this weekend, or if any sudden hawkish Fed surprise emerges from Warsh positioning, gold could swing bullish quickly. Conversely, if peace deal confirms fully AND equities continue to rally into SpaceX/tech strength, gold could break lower toward $4,150. Real yields direction (currently unclear) is the tiebreaker; a sharp rise in 10y TIPS would accelerate downside.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (risk-on unwind in progress); do not chase shorts yet. Wait for pullback retest of $4,250-$4,280 resistance or a 3-4h consolidation before considering short entry. If long, hold core position but reduce size until Warsh FOMC clarity emerges (Friday June 17 afternoon into Monday).

factors.name: Real yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS data in news window. Nominal 10y Treasury yields likely supported by stronger-than-expected Michigan consumer sentiment (48.9 vs 46.0 expected), which typically keeps real yields firm. This is a headwind for gold but not yet decisive.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: Iran peace deal chatter has softened USD across the day. Multiple sources note DXY losing ground on de-escalation hopes (oil down to $88/bbl, risk-on sentiment). Weaker USD is BULLISH for gold, but market is uncertain on deal credibility after Trump denied leaked terms as 'fake.'

factors.name: Geopolitical tension (Middle East)
factors.summary: US-Iran deal narrative has shifted twice today: morning optimism on peace talks → afternoon skepticism after Trump denounced leaked terms as 'dishonorable.' VP Vance also clarified no cash flows for signing. Reduced geopolitical risk premium has temporarily lifted gold intraday (tested $4,246 high), but credibility gaps are causing chop.

factors.name: Oil prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: Crude fell to $88/bbl on Iran deal hopes. Lower oil eases inflation expectations and reduces energy-cost safe-haven demand for gold. However, energy sanctions uncertainty lingers, capping the downside move in oil and limiting gold's downside.

factors.name: Risk sentiment (equities, VIX)
factors.summary: Broad US stock indices gave back some Thursday gains (Iran deal uncertainty resurfacing). Tech stocks rallied (SpaceX IPO euphoria, AMD +5.39%), but this is a 'risk-on' move that can suppress gold as investors rotate to growth. Mixed signals—no clear VIX data in window.

factors.name: Inflation expectations (near-term)
factors.summary: Michigan survey showed 1-year inflation at 4.6% (vs 4.8% prior) and 5-year at 3.4% (vs 3.9% prior), suggesting slight disinflation. This is modestly BULLISH for gold, but the move is already partially priced into today's action.

factors.name: Federal Reserve policy
factors.summary: No new FOMC decision or Fed speak in the last 24h window. No hawkish surprises, no dovish surprises. Status quo maintains a higher-for-longer bias that is slightly BEARISH for gold, but not acute.

factors.name: Other central banks (ECB, BoE)
factors.summary: ECB delivered a 25bp hike to 2.25% depo rate with more hawkish forecasts (per Nomura). BoE expected to hold at 3.75% with persistent inflation risks. Higher global real rates support USD and are a headwind for gold.

factors.name: Gold ETF flows & CB buying
factors.summary: No GLD, IAU, or central bank gold purchase data in this news window. No fresh structural bid or exodus signals.

factors.name: Competing safe-havens
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO euphoria and broader risk-on sentiment are pulling capital into equities and away from defensive assets. Bitcoin also rebounding (mentioned in news). Gold facing some flow leakage to risk assets.

screening.narrative: Gold rallied intraday on Iran peace-deal de-escalation chatter and weaker USD, testing $4,246 early. However, Trump's midday denial that leaked deal terms are 'fake' and VP Vance's comments on no cash transfers have muddied the geopolitical thesis, causing price to consolidate near $4,229. ECB's hawkish rate hike and stronger-than-expected US consumer sentiment have kept real yields supported, offsetting the DXY weakness. Risk sentiment is mixed: SpaceX IPO euphoria (risk-on) competes with Iran deal uncertainty (safe-haven). Gold is now in a chop zone, with neither bulls nor bears in clear control.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran peace deal signed/rejected (Trump says could happen 'this weekend' in Europe)
Strait of Hormuz sanctions clarification / oil flow restoration updates
Bank of England decision (next week; BoE expected to hold but listen for hawkish forward guidance)
UK CPI/labour data (next week; will influence BoE path and GBP/USD, impact on USD carry)
No major US economic data or FOMC announcements in next 24-48h (reduces near-term catalyst risk)
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran deal is formally announced and geopolitical risk premium collapses further, USD could strengthen sharply (reversing today's weakness) and oil could dive, potentially pushing gold lower toward $4,150–$4,170. Conversely, if deal talks fail or Trump withdraws, risk-off reversal could send gold back toward $4,280+. Equities (SpaceX euphoria) could also re-accelerate, pulling gold lower via yen carry-unwind and risk-on sentiment. ECB's hawkish surprise earlier today is a longer-term headwind not yet fully priced into gold.
screening.entry_guidance: Price is in PHASE 2 (intraday move in progress, already tested highs). Avoid chasing $4,246 resistance; wait for consolidation and either a break above with conviction or a pullback to $4,189–$4,210 support before fresh entries. Iran deal clarity is essential before committing size—event risk is too high for aggressive positioning.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran Deal)

factors.summary: SpaceX IPO (+25% debut, $1.77T valuation) and strong equity rally (Dow +0.6%, Bitcoin to $64,349 session high) signal risk-on appetite. DJIA near 51,200 suggests capital rotation away from safe-havens into growth/speculative assets.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)

factors.name: Oil Prices (Inflation Input)
factors.summary: WTI fell sharply toward $84 and Brent briefly touched multi-month lows after Iran peace deal headlines (per MarketWatch 16:55). Lower oil reduces near-term inflation expectations and removes commodity bid from gold.

factors.name: Inflation Data / CPI/PCE
factors.summary: No fresh inflation prints in news window. Next major release likely weeks ahead; social security COLA estimate of 4.7% for 2027 mentioned but not market-moving today.

factors.name: Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: Gold up +0.74% over 24h and +0.25% in 4h; intraday high of $4,246 suggests buyers present. However, consolidation around $4,200-4,246 range indicates hesitation ahead of peace deal resolution.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a near-term tug-of-war between two conflicting forces: (1) improving geopolitical sentiment (US-Iran peace deal imminent) and risk-on equity/crypto rally (SpaceX IPO, Bitcoin +0.6% to $64k) are weighing on safe-haven demand and pushing oil lower toward $84/bbl, which reduces inflation bid; (2) conversely, intraday strength to $4,246 and modest 24h gains suggest some institutional dip-buying or short-covering. The 4h and 24h moves are small (+0.25%, +0.74%), reflecting indecision. Prior bias history (SELL 7/10 on 06-12 08:23, then NEUTRAL on subsequent reads at 12:17 and 16:16) shows the market has trimmed conviction since the morning bearish call.

screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidation scenario: (1) If US-Iran peace deal COLLAPSES, geopolitical premium reverts sharply and gold rallies above $4,300. (2) If SpaceX rally reverses and equity market rolls over (VIX spikes), safe-haven flows and real yield repricing could send gold to $4,400+. (3) If incoming CPI/inflation data surprises hot, real yields rise and DXY strengthens, both bearish for gold below $4,200. (4) BoJ hike on Tuesday could trigger yen carry unwind and USD weakness, which would support gold—monitor that closely.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (intraday consolidation after modest up move). Avoid chasing above $4,246 resistance; wait for pullback to $4,220 support or a fresh headline catalyst (Iran deal finalized OR market risk-off) before committing. If peace deal confirmed, expect retest of $4,200 and potential break below; if deal fails, watch for $4,300 resistance as first upside target.

factors.name: Real yields (10y TIPS)

factors.summary: No new FOMC decision or Powell speak in past 24h. Focus on upcoming week's central bank decisions (Fed, BoJ, BoE, RBA per FXStreet). BoJ 25 bps hike widely anticipated Tuesday; no major rate-cut signals from Fed yet.

factors.name: Inflation expectations & prints
factors.summary: Social Security COLA estimate of 4.7% for 2027 noted, but this is backward-looking. No fresh CPI/PCE/PPI prints in news. Inflation-linked trade is quiet; focus shifts to peace-trade narrative (lower energy = disinflationary).

factors.name: Risk sentiment & equity strength
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO massive success (shares +25%, market cap >$2T), retail mania evident. US major indices closed higher on Iran deal optimism per InvestingLive. Risk-on mood pressures gold as rotation out of safe-havens into equities/tech likely.

factors.name: Central bank gold buying (structural bid)
factors.summary: No new reserve manager flows reported in news. Historical CB buying remains structural support, but no fresh catalyst to accelerate or decelerate buying in this window.

factors.name: Gold price action (4h/24h momentum)
factors.summary: 4h change -0.04%, 24h change +0.66%; price consolidating above $4,200 per FXStreet comment. Minimal intraday momentum; move is shallow and lacking conviction. Price has *not* decisively followed either direction yet.

factors.name: Upcoming event risk (next 72h)
factors.summary: Heavy central bank calendar next week: BoJ Tue, BoE, RBA, plus US Retail Sales and FOMC. High uncertainty = potential chop and mean-reversion trades. Markets often consolidate into major events rather than trend.

screening.narrative: Gold cleared $4,200 Friday on peace-deal optimism but lacks follow-through conviction. The Iran-US deal framework removes Middle East risk premium that had been supporting safe-haven flows and oil prices. Simultaneously, equity markets rally hard (SpaceX mania, risk-on mood) and DXY traders turn most bullish since Feb 2025, both headwinds for gold. Real yields and Fed policy remain in neutral-to-hold mode with no major easing signals. Next week's central bank calendar (BoJ, BoE, RBA, FOMC) will be the dominant catalyst; markets often chop sideways into such events rather than trend decisively.
catalysts_ahead: BoJ rate decision & hike (Tue 2026-06-15) → USD/JPY weakness would pressure gold
BoE and RBA policy decisions (likely week of 16-17 June) → further FX/yield moves
US Retail Sales data (likely next week) → inflation narrative shift
FOMC decision & Powell presser (likely Tue-Wed next week) → largest catalyst; if hawkish, BEARISH gold; if dovish, BULLISH
Iran peace deal signing finalized (Trump says 'coming days') → if signed, geopolitical premium fully priced out
screening.risk_warning: If FOMC signals earlier-than-expected rate cuts OR if real yields fall sharply, gold would flip BULLISH fast. Conversely, if Iran deal talks collapse or Middle East tensions re-ignite, risk-on unwinds and gold rallies on safe-haven bid. Geopolitical risk is elevated despite optimistic headlines — any breakdown in talks could reverse the de-escalation narrative within hours.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is shallow and choppy; wait for either a retest of $4,200 support (pullback entry if FOMC is dovish) or a break above $4,260 (breakout confirmation). Given next week's event risk, do not chase: bias remains neutral into central bank decisions. Consider building long positions only on either (a) hard FOMC dovish surprise or (b) a fresh geopolitical shock.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk premium (Iran deal)
factors.summary: US-Iran deal framework reportedly close to signing; Strait of Hormuz reopening expected, which reduces safe-haven demand and geopolitical tail risk. FXStreet reports gold clearing $4,200 on deal optimism, but this is a de-risking event that typically pressures gold as investors rotate to risk assets.

factors.name: Oil prices (risk-off indicator)
factors.summary: US crude fell below $85 as Iran deal prospects improved; lower oil reduces inflation expectations and removes a key gold support pillar. Commodity weakness alongside equities strength (SpaceX IPO euphoria) suggests risk-on sentiment against gold.

factors.name: Real yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS data in news window. Nominal 10y Treasury likely stable; real yields probably near recent range. Without a directional catalyst, this remains neutral.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: FXStreet reports DXY lost 0.27% during the week to ~99.80 as markets prepared for heavy central bank calendar. Modest weakness is not a clear headwind for gold; mixed signal.

factors.name: Inflation expectations / economic data
factors.summary: No fresh CPI, PCE, or NFP in window. MarketWatch headline on Social Security COLA rising to 4.7% (2027 est.) suggests inflation stickiness, but this is future-dated and lacks immediate punch. Data calendar heavy next week (FOMC, Retail Sales, NFP implied).

factors.name: Risk sentiment / equity strength
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO at $2.2T market cap, Elon Musk becoming world's first trillionaire, broad equity enthusiasm on Iran deal and risk-on trades. Strong equities typically compete with gold's safe-haven bid; retail and institutional capital flowing into growth/tech assets away from bullion.

factors.name: Central bank policy (Fed)
factors.summary: No new FOMC decision, dot plot, or Powell speech in this window. Kevin Warsh (new Fed Chairman per BBC) prefers 'Chairman' title but no policy signal yet. Rate expectations unchanged absent new data.

factors.name: Gold ETF flows
factors.summary: No GLD or IAU holdings data in news window. Institutional positioning unknown; cannot assess.

factors.name: Physical demand seasonality
factors.summary: June is not a peak demand month (off-season for weddings/CNY). Seasonal tailwind absent.

factors.name: Price action: 24h and 4h momentum
factors.summary: Price +0.66% in 24h is modest; 4h change -0.04% shows consolidation or slight pullback. Move not extended, but positive 24h bias conflicts with bearish macro narrative (geopolitical risk off, equity rally, oil down).

screening.narrative: Gold is consolidating near $4,240 as a de-risking trade unfolds. The headline driver is optimistic progress on a US-Iran deal framework, expected to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and lower geopolitical tail risk—traditionally a headwind for bullion. Simultaneously, SpaceX's record $2.2T IPO and broader risk-on sentiment (equities rallying, oil selling off) are pulling capital away from safe-haven assets and into growth plays. Real yields appear stable, and the USD is only modestly weaker. Gold has rallied 0.66% in 24h but lacks conviction given macro cross-currents. Prior three reads (16:16, 19:03, 21:42 UTC) all NEUTRAL; this is a sustained neutral-to-slightly-bearish hold, not a fresh reversal.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision (week of June 17-18 implied, exact date TBD—check calendar)
Retail Sales data (likely mid-week)
NFP (first Friday of July, but early warnings via ADP mid-week)
Iran deal signing (Trump admin says 'coming days, not 100% certain'—if signed, likely bearish gold)
Powell/Waller/Williams speeches (watch for rate-hold vs. hike bias)
PCE/Core PCE inflation print (likely mid-to-late June)
BoJ, BoE, ECB decisions (FXStreet mentions heavy central bank calendar next week)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal falls apart or geopolitical tensions spike (Israel escalation, Russia-Ukraine flare-up), gold could pivot sharply higher as safe-haven flows return. Conversely, if inflation data surprises hot or Fed signals hawkish hold/hike, real yields could rise, crushing gold. SpaceX euphoria and broad risk-on could extend if earnings beat or AI narrative stays strong—further headwind. Monitor DXY closely; if dollar strengthens sharply on hawkish Fed pivot, gold loses dual support.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 consolidation post-risk-off; wait for clearer catalyst (Iran deal signed, inflation print, FOMC) before taking a directional view. If bearish thesis holds, consider short on retest of $4,260 resistance with stop above $4,290. If bullish setup (deal collapses), buy dip to $4,200-$4,220 support. Current level is chop territory—avoid.
